description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring alternating parallel stripes of light gray stone and deep black grooves. The grooves are evenly spaced and run the full length of the block, creating a visually striking pattern reminiscent of urban tactile paving or anti-skid surfaces. The sides of the block are solid black, giving it a bold, modern appearance. The block is thick and robust, suggesting it is designed to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. The surface texture appears rough and unpolished, typical of concrete or granite used in outdoor settings. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering.
description: A thick rectangular stone block with alternating light gray and black stripes on top and solid black sides, resembling urban tactile paving.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped top surface with the stripes running horizontally and the solid black front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the block and the vertical orientation of the stripes, with the solid black side face visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the striped top surface and solid black rear face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the depth and vertical stripes, with the solid black side face.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or pedestrian crossing zone
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surfaces appear clean and well-maintained, with no moss, chips, or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous barrier or tactile paving system
